## Mochi AI Architecture Redesign — Execution Pipeline, json-render, Background Mode

Implement the full Mochi AI architecture redesign plan covering:
1. Intent detection for background mode (user says "do this in the background" → no navigation)
2. Navigation controller abstraction (extract inline navigation logic into pure function)
3. Execution strategy registry (plug-and-play pattern for adding new execution modes)
4. json-render integration as a content fence (streaming + generating UI)
5. Post-stream variable registry (centralized variable detection after streaming)

The redesign makes the system modular, extensible, and deterministic by introducing background mode, a navigation controller, json-render as a new content fence type, a variable registry, and a strategy pattern so new execution modes are plug-and-play without touching multiple files.

1. When we select an element/section/component/frame on the page canvas, it has an ID which Mochi AI chat should know about. AI needs to be aware of the currently selected element.
2. When we tell AI to create anything while something is selected, it should create it above/before the selected element by default. User should explicitly say in the prompt if they want different placement.
3. If nothing is selected, AI should create the component on the canvas (not on the page element), so the user can drag and drop and arrange it however they want.

When nothing is selected, create elements as floating root elements (not children of the page) so the user can drag-and-drop them into position. ## Mochi AI - Tool Streaming & Background Mode Awareness

When introducing the available tools to the AI, each tool description should clearly indicate whether it supports streaming mode (realtime content generation) and/or background mode (non-navigating silent execution). For any tool that supports either mode, the AI must ask the user upfront whether they want it done in the background or in realtime. This prevents the AI from hallucinating navigation decisions and ensures it correctly navigates to the relevant page when streaming is required.

## Contract Variable Load-Time Parser

Contract variables are currently not working properly. We need a parsing system for contract variables, similar to how input fields are handled.

Right now, input fields are inserted in the contract builder using a format like [CONTRACT_VAR=...], where the values are stored within that structured pattern. Reuse this format and build a parser that runs when the contract builder loads.

When the contract builder opens, the parser should:
1. Detect any contract variables or input fields created by the AI.
2. Identify broken or pending variables that were not properly initialized.
3. Parse the stored format and reconstruct them correctly, ensuring they are properly built and saved even if something previously failed.

This is necessary because the AI sometimes creates contract variables during real-time streaming, and the system currently tries to parse them in real time, which is not reliable. When the AI performs tasks in the background (such as creating a contract in the background), those variables are not parsed, and they end up being stored as plain yellow ID placeholders in the contract instead of fully functional variables.

Implementing a load-time parser will ensure that all variables are consistently reconstructed and stored correctly, regardless of whether the contract was generated in real time or in the background. This issue happened some time ago but in today's testing this thing never happened. It sometimes happened during background process but in real-time it happened once or twice.

## Fix Dynamic Page Template Lookup Bug - CMS Product Gallery

There's a bug when connecting CMS multi-image gallery to a product gallery element in the website builder. The dynamic page template lookup is failing with 404. The debug logs show that the raw page lookup finds the page correctly (with correct slug, cmsTableId, websiteId), but then the "Website pages for previewId" lookup returns websiteFound: false. The getDynamicPageTemplate preview result comes back as found: false, so it fails with "template not found or no cmsTableId". The issue seems to be in how the template is resolved — the page exists but the lookup by previewId is not finding the website.

## Drag and Drop Broken Inside Master Components (Mobile Breakpoint Collision)

User noticed that dragging children inside a master/local component causes elements to glitch and move incorrectly - specifically affecting elements in the mobile breakpoint renderer. The root cause was that document.querySelector for data-element-id would match elements inside the BreakpointMobileFrame (which renders the same elements with the same IDs), causing wrong sibling positions, wrong sort calculations, and transforms applied to the wrong elements.

## Remove duplicate border properties from property registry

**Prompt:** Continue fixing the duplicate border properties in the property registry. The individual `borderWidth`, `borderColor`, `borderStyle` properties on button-type elements (button, add-to-cart-button, cart) are dead code because `computeButtonContentStyles` uses `__borderConfig` via `BorderControl` for borders, not individual CSS border properties. Remove these duplicates and ensure the registry is consistent across all button-type elements while keeping legitimate properties like `borderRadius` and `padding`.

## Template System — Full Architecture & Template Library

**Prompt:**

The next feature we need to build is a very complex but important feature called a Template System. This allows orgs to create templates out of something and make them available in a template library.

This includes different categories of templates:
- Feature-specific: website templates (single or bundle), email templates (single or bundle)
- Org templates / Blueprints: entire systems that can be duplicated and installed within another org

This is a dangerous system and needs careful analysis for security and user experience. Users should know exactly what type of template they are about to install into their organization.

**Most Important: Reusable Architecture**
The template system might appear in different UIs — e.g., a template library in the sidebar, or within a website builder when the user clicks "create from template." This means we need a single source of truth Modal that works everywhere inside the application. Layout similar to the settings modal in the website builder: left side shows template categories (Emails, Websites, Automations, Blueprints), right side shows content.

Users can download AND upload templates to the library.

**Dependency Detection & Bundling**
Sometimes features are tied to other features. Example: an automation being converted to a template has a form trigger (requires a specific form) and a send-email action (requires an email template). The user should see a beautiful UI alert saying their template is connected to other features. Options:
1. Yes — bundle everything in
2. No — only bundle the main item (may cause errors, user is aware)
3. Choose Which — user selects which dependency elements to bundle

Examples of dependency scenarios:
- Website with payment form connected to product/price + CMS table
- If "No": only website bundled, CMS and products excluded, may cause errors
- If "Yes": everything bundled minus dangerous/protected data
- If "Choose": user picks specific dependencies to include

**Security & Data Protection Rules**
- Confidential data NEVER included: leads, messages, invoices, customer data, team member personal info, payment details
- Calendar mounted to specific team member: team member info stripped out
- Blueprints (full org copy): team info, payment details, lead info, all confidential data filtered out
- Invoice templates may be included, but actual customer invoices never

**Download/Install Options**
When installing a bundle, users can opt out of specific dependencies. If they install a website but skip the CMS, that's their choice.

**No Selling for Now** — templates are free to share/download for now. Selling comes later.

**Template Origin Tracking (Anti-Plagiarism)**
When something is installed as a template, it must have metadata — a secret hash or similar marker. This prevents users from re-bundling downloaded templates and reselling them. If a user downloads a template, all its pieces are marked. If they try to bundle any of those marked items, the system blocks it saying "this is part of a template and cannot be bundled." Even if they duplicate the item to try to hack around it, the marker should persist/propagate to duplicates.

**Prompt:** Fix my build please.

## Template system 
 The next feature we need to build is a VERY complex feature, but is very    
  important. This is called a template system. This allows orgs to create      
  templates out of something, and make them available in a template library.   
  This includes different category of templates, we have feature specific      
  like website templates (Single or as a bundle), email templates (single or   
  as a a bundle) and also org templates. They can sell entire systems! that    
  means one system can be duplicated and installed withing another org. THIS   
  IS OBVIOUSLY a very dangerous system and needs careful analysis of this      
  feature and feature implementation. MORE importantly, the Most important     
  part is Security, and also the User experience. They should know wxactly     
  what type of template they are about to install into their organization.     
  The focus here is reusable architecture! This is the MOST MOST important     
  part right after security. The reason is because, This template system       
  might be in different type of UI's for example I might have a template       
  library in the sidebar where users an install things. Or I might even have   
  it within a different UI, lets say for example a website builder when the    
  user clicks on create from template. This means we need to hav a single      
  source of truth Modal! And that modal works everywhere inside the            
  application. For the layout of this modal, you can use something just like   
  the settings modal in the website builder. On the left side, show template   
  categories, like emails, Websites, Automations, Blueprints (These are the    
  full account systems / organization templates with multiple things in it).   
  Users can obviosuly create these templates bundle them and upload them to    
  the template library. So users can not only download they can also upload.   
  Now another Very VEry important thing to keep in mind is, SOMETIMES, other   
  features might be tied up with other features. So what this means is. Lets   
  say an automation is going to be converted to a template by an org and uploaded to the template library. HOWEVER, this automation has actions like a form trigger which requires a SPECIFIC form, and also has another action which is send an email which has a beautiful email template. Since the user is bundling this automation as a template the they should see a beautiful UI through an alert or whatever, that says your template is connected to other features as well. Would you like to bundle those into this template as well? The options they will have is something like Yes bundle them in, No, And Choose Which to attach. With this option the user gets to select which dependency elements are needed to be bundled into this and then that will get bundled into the blueprint. So let me give you another example, user bundles a website, website comes with a payment form that is connected to a product with a specific price and the website is also connected to a specific CMS table. Now because the website is connected to other dependency features, the user sees this message. Now lets look at the different scenarios here:
* If the user said no, then only the website is bundled in. That means nothing else will be connected. The CMS will not be bundled in; the products won't be bundled in, so this can obviously lead to errors, but the user is well aware of all of this now.
* If the user clicked yes, then everything will be bundled in minus any dangerous protected data. For example, if there was, in a different example, let's just say there was a lead or some leads attached, these dangerous data will not be bundled in with the template, obviously.
* I don't think I need to explain this to you. For example, like messages or anything that is confidential, these are not going to be part of this architecture at all. Let's say invoices. A customer's invoice cannot be sent as a template, but an invoice template may be. We are not adding invoices anyway, but I think you get my point.
* Let's say another example is calendars. If calendars were added onto the website, now what if that calendar was mounted to a specific team member? When you're duplicating this calendar for that user, you are not going to add that user's information into the template, because that is dangerous, that is confidential breach.
* The same thing for blueprints as a whole. If you're copying a blueprint of a full account, things like team information, payment details, all of these need to be filtered out. Lead information, all of the import and confidential information that cannot be transferred should be filtered.
Regardless, let's continue. Let's look at everything.
* If they say no, then only the website will be there, and everything could break, but it will successfully create a template out of it.
* If they say yes, then it will automatically bundle everything.
* If they say select, then they will have a beautiful UI UX that will ask them which specific dependency they want to bundle into this template, and they can select.
They can check everything, and that will be bundled in. Now anyone who downloads it will obviously get all of this, and they will also see which of these are the main template and what it comes with as an addition.

Let's say if they're trying to download something, but that download comes as a bundle to try to install it into their orc, they can have the option to also opt out of the dependency. If they installed the website but they don't install the CMS, it's up to them; they can choose that.

This is one thing I want you to also keep in mind. For now nobody is selling these templates. We look at that later on in the future, but the main point is just this now.

What's the next most important part? When something is installed as a template, or when the blueprint or a bundle, whatever, is installed, it has to have some kind of metadata added on to it, sort of like a secret hash or something. This helps us prevent users from sharing these with other people.

What that means is I'll give you an example. If I just downloaded a template and all its bits and pieces are in my app, if we don't track this I can rebundle their shit and then resell it in the marketplace, which is a security breach and it's dangerous and it's illegal and can plagiarize. We need to make sure that if there's something installed that thing that is installed has a mark on it in some way. If the user tries to bundle that, that will say this is part of a template and cannot be bundled at all even if they try to duplicate that inside.

Let's say they downloaded a form and they duplicate the form again, trying to hack the system; that will still bypass that. Keep this in mind.
Now before you proceed with this feature, I want you to create a document that mentions all these features as a checklist, not technical requirements, just all the features, and do not miss even a single one. Then as we build, we can keep track of this if I need to or if my dev team needs to in the future.

## Sync Changes — Handle Soft-Deleted and Permanently Deleted Features

Outlier scenario: User installs a template with 5 elements (website, chat widget, CMS schemas, products, etc.), then deletes one of them from the CRM, and tries to sync changes. Some features have soft delete (still in DB with deletedAt), some are hard deleted (gone forever).

Problems:
- If something is soft deleted and sync tries to update it, the user thinks it updated but it's hidden — silent failure.
- If something is permanently deleted, sync would break trying to update a non-existent record.

Solution:
- If a feature was soft deleted (has deletedAt set), restore it by clearing deletedAt before applying the sync update. That way it comes back and the user sees the updated version.
- If a feature was permanently deleted (not in DB at all), mark it as missing in the sync result so the user knows it couldn't be synced.
- Show the user clear feedback about what was synced, what was restored, and what was missing.


---

## Template Installation — 3 Critical Bugs

### Bug 1: Component Instance IDs Not Remapped
When installing a website template, the local components get duplicated with new IDs, but all component instances in the canvasData still point to the OLD component IDs. This causes "component not found" errors in the website builder. The canvasData component instance elements need their componentId references remapped to the new local component IDs.

### Bug 2: CMS List Component Settings Not Mapped
Inside the CMS list component on the canvas, the CMS table is correctly matched and data is duplicated. However:
- The item template (component template) reference is not being remapped to the new component ID
- The two exposed props bindings are not being remapped either
- The data binding settings inside the CMS list element are left pointing to old IDs

### Bug 3: Product/Price Currency Issue
Products and prices installed from a template are stored with incorrect currency. For example, a template originally in USD gets installed on an org that uses AED. The price details show AED but the display shows $. The installation might not be going through the correct router/service that handles Stripe currency properly. Need to investigate what data is stored for the organization "home town" using the Neon MCP.

As a result, the header blocks the headers in the builder. Fix this bug by getting inspiration from how the app layout handles this. Do not break what was already there or duplicate the logic again because that will lead to code drift.

## Template Library — Public Route + Complete UI/UX Redesign

### Overview
Move template browsing out of the dashboard modal into a reusable server component that works on a public route (anyone can browse templates as a teaser) AND inside the dashboard. The functionality is perfect — this is purely a UI/UX overhaul focused on minimalism.

### Layout Requirements
- Containerized two-column layout centered on the page
- Left side: narrow filter sidebar (categories)
- Right side: template cards grid
- Minimal, low contrast design — muted backgrounds, subtle fonts
- Follow the same color/contrast feel as the automation builder sidebar (background + font contrast in both light and dark mode)
- Use shadcn Breadcrumbs for navigation when clicking into a template detail

### Template Cards — Feature-Specific Rendering
- Website templates: render like the websites list page (with thumbnail support)
- Email templates: render like the email templates list page
- Product templates: render with product image
- Automation templates: keep simple/generic (no special rendering)
- Each card design should stand out but remain minimal and low contrast

### Template Detail Page
- When clicking a template, show full details on the page (not a modal)
- Breadcrumb navigation at the top for back navigation
- Show description, items included, metadata
- Description should use the Lexical editor (already built in the app) so creators can add rich content like images, formatted text, etc.

### Uploading Templates (Dashboard Only)
- Template creation/upload stays inside the dashboard
- Improve the upload UI/UX — make it less complicated and cleaner
- Allow thumbnail uploads for websites
- Use the Lexical editor for description input
- Render feature selection like how features appear in their own list pages (e.g., websites shown like the websites page)

### Reusable Component Architecture
- Build the browse/detail views as a reusable component that works anywhere
- Public route renders it for anonymous access
- Dashboard can also render it for authenticated users
- Server component for fast rendering

## Template Library UI/UX Redesign — Public Route + Reusable Components

**Prompt:** We need to make awesome enhancements to the UI/UX and design for the templates so everything looks stunning and amazing. The current modal-based browsing experience needs to be replaced with a proper page-based approach:

1. Move template browsing to a public route so anyone can see and browse templates without being logged in. Create a reusable component that can be rendered on the public route and also inside the dashboard.
2. Use a containerized two-column layout — narrow filter sidebar on the left, content grid on the right. Follow the automation builder aesthetic with minimalism, low contrast, and muted backgrounds.
3. Feature-specific card rendering — website template cards should look like the websites page cards (portrait 3:4 aspect ratio), email cards like the email page, automation cards with a horizontal layout and accent, and generic cards for everything else.
4. Template detail should be a full page with breadcrumbs navigation (not a modal panel). Two-column layout with rich description on the left and sticky install card on the right.
5. Use the Lexical rich text editor for template descriptions during creation and display them in read-only mode on the detail page.
6. Template creation and upload stays dashboard-only with improved UX — use tabs (Browse Library, My Templates, Installed) instead of the old modal sidebar.
7. Use server component architecture for the public pages with fast rendering. Delete the old modal-based files.


---

## Template Library Route Conflict Fix — Public vs Dashboard Routes

**Prompt:** You cannot have two parallel pages that resolve to the same path. The dashboard templates route under (protected)/(dashboard-layout)/templates and the public route under (public)/templates both resolve to /templates since route groups don't add URL segments. Call the public route "Templates" at /templates and rename the dashboard route to "Marketplace" at /marketplace.

## Marketplace URL Routing Enhancement (Parallel Routing)

**Prompt:**
We need to make an enhancement to our marketplace. Right now, the first enhancement we need to make is the URL. At the moment, all these different marketplace elements do not have URL configurations. That means users cannot share these templates; they cannot open them differently. It's all just not working properly.

The strategy we're going to implement here is known as parallel routing. That means the same routing architecture will also be parallelly loaded inside our dashboard and also in the public version as well. Inside the dashboard, it's going to be marketplace/whatever, and in the public version it can be templates/whatever or marketplace/whatever.

This is good because users can share templates amongst each other. That is the first thing: implement this change and be careful to not violate Next.js principles of parallel routing.


---

## Update Public Template Routes with basePath and backHref Props

Update the public template routes to use the new basePath and backHref props. Modify the templates page to pass basePath="/templates" to TemplateBrowseView, and modify the template detail page to pass backHref="/templates" to TemplateDetailView. Use the Edit tool for both modifications.

## Dashboard Marketplace Route Structure

Create the dashboard marketplace route structure with proper sub-routes. The base path is `/marketplace/` under the dashboard layout.

1. Create `layout.tsx` as a server component following the settings layout pattern with ContentLayout, SectionHeader (title "Marketplace"), and MarketplaceNav
2. Create `_components/marketplace-nav.tsx` as a client component with tabs (Browse, My Templates, Installed) following the SettingsNav border-b-2 tab pattern, plus a Create button that opens a Dialog with TemplateCreateWizard
3. Create `_components/marketplace-browse-content.tsx` as a client component wrapper for TemplateBrowseView with basePath="/marketplace"
4. Simplify `page.tsx` to just render MarketplaceBrowseContent
5. Create `my-templates/page.tsx` and `_components/my-templates-content.tsx` wrapping MyTemplatesView in TemplateLibraryProvider
6. Create `installed/page.tsx` and `_components/installed-content.tsx` wrapping InstalledTemplatesView in TemplateLibraryProvider
7. Create `[templateId]/page.tsx` as a server component for template detail, following the public template detail page pattern but with backHref="/marketplace"
8. Delete old `_components/templates-page-content.tsx` since it is replaced by the layout + sub-routes

## Bug: Hardcoded $ Currency Instead of Dynamic Platform Currency

I noticed a terrible bug in our codebase. Right now I hardcoded $ as the platform's currency instead of using the currency the our Stripe account returns because initially I wanted to launch in the US but now I'm thinking of launching in a different country. So this means this calls for another currency system (we have already built something similar for organization! we just have to create something for platform level too) and that way we can DYNAMICALLY use this currency that dynamically comes from the Stripe account of the platform. Please go ahead and plan this carefully because you will need to change this in different places, including the upgrade modal, including the fees charged to the user, including the onboarding process, and anything else in our application that from a PLATFORM level where we are displaying prices as well.


---

## Template Pricing, Purchasing & Approval — Implementation with Platform Currency

The platform currency system was just built (platform-currency.service.ts, PlatformCurrencyProvider). Templates are sold through the platform, so the currency that renders for template prices is the platform currency. The architecture is already built for platform currency to be dynamic — no need to save currency on the Template model. The platform currency is fetched from the platform Stripe account's default_currency via stripe.accounts.retrieve() and cached at request level.

Key files for platform currency:
- src/services/platform-currency.service.ts — getPlatformCurrency() with React.cache()
- src/components/providers/platform-currency-provider.tsx — PlatformCurrencyProvider + usePlatformCurrency() + usePlatformCurrencySymbol()
- src/trpc/routers/features.ts — getPlatformCurrency endpoint

Now implement the full template pricing, purchasing, and approval system:
1. Pricing step in create wizard (free/paid toggle + price input using platform currency)
2. Paid template approval workflow — paid templates go to PENDING_APPROVAL, portal admin reviews
3. Portal templates page for approval with auto-approve toggle
4. Email notification to platform admin on submission
5. Buy Now button for paid templates (buyers must be on paid plan)
6. Payment dialog using CardElement
7. Transaction recording on template owner's connected account with platform fees
8. Use a TEAM to complete this


---

## Template Pricing, Purchasing, and Approval System — Backend Implementation

**WHAT**: Backend implementation for the template pricing, purchasing, and portal approval system. This includes types, context updates, service layer (template approval workflow + purchase service), tRPC router updates (pricing schemas + purchase endpoint + portal approval endpoints), portal permissions for templates, and approval email notifications.

**PROMPT**: Build the backend foundation for template pricing and purchasing. Templates can have an optional price (in cents). When a paid template is published, it goes to PENDING_APPROVAL status instead of PUBLISHED — unless auto-approve is enabled in portal settings. Portal admins can approve or reject templates. Buyers on paid plans can purchase templates which creates a Stripe PaymentIntent on the seller's connected account with platform fee, records the transaction, and installs the template. Free templates continue to publish immediately. Add portal permissions for template management, approval email notifications, and all the tRPC endpoints needed.

Use usePlatformCurrency for all price formatting - never hardcode dollar signs or USD.

## UpgradeModal Not Opening from Template Install Dialog — Root Cause Fix

**Prompt:** The UpgradeModal was not opening when clicking "Upgrade Plan" from the template install dialog gate violation banner. After extensive debugging with Playwright MCP, the root cause was found: the `organizationId` prop passed from the server component page was `undefined` (the server-side `session.activeOrganizationId` was not set). The condition `{organizationId && <UpgradeModal>}` always evaluated to false. Fixed by using the client-side `useActiveOrganizationId()` hook as a fallback (`resolvedOrgId = organizationId || clientOrgId`). Also cleaned up all debug code including temporary test buttons, console logs, and unused imports.

## Fix 500 Error: Subscription Created Webhook During Upgrade Flow

**Prompt:** Found a bug in the payment flow. When upgrading a Free plan member to a Free trial for the next plan, a 500 error is thrown. The `customer.subscription.created` webhook handler fails with "Invalid subscription metadata: missing userId or studioData" at `stripe-webhook.service.ts:91`. The handler expects `userId` and `studioData` in the subscription metadata, but the upgrade flow (payment.upgradeOrganization) doesn't set `studioData` because the organization already exists. Need to fix the webhook handler to handle both onboarding (new org with studioData) and upgrade (existing org, no studioData) subscription creation flows.


---

## Bug: 500 Error on Free-to-Trial Upgrade + Success Shown Despite Pending Payment

**Prompt:** Found a bug in the payment flow. Upgraded a Free plan member to a Free trial for the next plan. Got a 500 error thrown: `customer.subscription.created` webhook fails with "Invalid subscription metadata: missing userId or studioData" at `stripe-webhook.service.ts:91`. The payment was also pending in Stripe. But somehow the frontend showed "success your payment went through." How is the frontend showing success if the realtime event is fired only on actual payment completion? Need to research this bug deeply - understand the full flow from upgrade mutation through webhook through frontend success state.

## Critical Bug Investigation: Organization Deleted on Subscription Cancellation

I was using prodigiestesting2@gmail.com who owns the Web Prodigies organization. While editing in the website builder, I suddenly got an error saying "user does not have permissions to edit this." After refreshing, the Web Prodigies organization was completely deleted. 

Looking at the DB, prodigiestesting7 is listed as the owner. Stripe logs show the subscription for prodigiestesting2 was automatically canceled (customer.subscription.deleted event). The webhook at mochidev.net/api/stripe/webhook received a 200 OK response, and the local listener also got it.

I need to understand exactly WHY the organization got deleted when the subscription was canceled. The account deletion should not happen silently. I also didn't see any warning message in the dashboard about the account being deleted after cancellation. The webhook may not have been running to deliver that warning.

We need to research this thoroughly first — trace the exact code path from subscription.deleted webhook to organization deletion — before implementing any fixes.


---

## Fix Destructive Subscription Deletion + Portal Organization Filtering

The handleSubscriptionDeleted function hard-deletes the entire organization when a subscription is canceled. This is catastrophically wrong. Instead, we should downgrade the user to the free plan when their subscription is canceled.

Additionally, in the portal page, we need to show organizations in categories through a filter:
- Trialing (includes free plan users)
- Active (only paid plans)  
- Cancelling (subscription set to cancel at period end but not yet ended)
- Churned (someone whose paid plan has ended — subscription.deleted came from Stripe)

Implement this feature and make sure there is no other self-destruct path in the application where organization deletion can happen from webhook events.


---

## Fix Subscription Cancellation Self-Destruct Bug + Portal Organization Filtering

Something really bad happened. I was using prodigiestesting2@gmail.com who owns Web Prodigies organization, and suddenly randomly I got an error in the website builder as I was editing - it said user does not have permissions to edit this. I refreshed and the Web Prodigies organization just got deleted. Stripe logs showed customer.subscription.deleted event fired. The webhook handler was doing a hard delete of the entire organization - that is SUPER destructive.

Instead what we should do is downgrade them to the free plan. Even though through our Env we can hide free plan, it doesn't matter because internally we have access to free plan. Let us use this Free plan here and downgrade the user to the free plan when their subscription is cancelled.

THEN we need to do something else here - in the portal page, we need to show the organizations in categories through a filter for: Trialing (technically this is also the free plan included in here), Active (only paid plans), Cancelling, Churned (someone whose paid plan has ended - subscription ended came from stripe basically).

Please implement this feature and once done please make sure there is no self destruct path inside our application where this can happen again.

## Navbar Hoisting Bug Fix — No Extra Section, Centered Navbars
The hoisted navbar fix is creating an extra section/space on top of the page. Its being placed OUTSIDE the page content div (above the background area). If the page element has a background image, the navbar sits above it with empty space. Also the navbar is not centered — the container max-width constraint is not being applied to the hoisted navbar. Fix: move hoisted navbars INSIDE the page content div (not above it) and ensure full-width centered rendering.




---

## Navbar Hoisting Root Cause — Stop Creating Extra Sections
The navbar hoisting approach is fundamentally flawed. Even when placed inside a container/section by accident, it creates an extra section on top of the page. The hoisting itself is the problem — pulling elements out of their natural position and rendering them separately always creates layout disruption. Need a completely different approach that fixes sticky positioning WITHOUT hoisting navbars out of their containers. Fix the root cause: make sticky work in-place regardless of nesting depth.

## Instance Component Canvas Sync Bug

I noticed the instance component sync bug only happens on the canvas - preview mode works perfectly fine. The canvas rendering of instance components doesn't perfectly sync child element styles with the master component. Fix this canvas-specific rendering issue.


---

## Fix Component Instance Style Sync Bug

There are issues with instance components in the website builder. Instance components don't perfectly sync child element styles. This should never happen. If it's happening it means the code is traversing through elements and manually doing things, when in reality an instance component is literally a COPY of the master component minus the properties that are exposed. Please simplify this to make sure there is always 100% sync. Some properties seen glitching are things like width and layout related properties. Specifically, fit content height and flexbox related properties don't work on instance components. On the preview mode it renders almost correctly (only width doesn't apply) but on canvas it completely forgets all styling.



---

## Navbar Z-Index Too Low When Stuck
The sticky navbar hides behind some sections. It needs a really high z-index by default, not just when stuck. Fix the z-index to always be high enough to stay on top of all page content.




---

## Navbar Z-Index Stacking Context Fix
The navbar goes behind other frames/sections because z-index only applies within the parent stacking context. When the navbar is inside a frame, the frame z-index traps it. Need to elevate all ancestor frames z-index when the navbar is stuck so it renders above everything.




---

## Navbar Sticky — Ditch JS, Use CSS-Only Solution
The JavaScript transform-based sticky approach feels janky and non-performant. It should be a simple CSS-only solution that just works. Find a proper CSS approach — no scroll listeners, no transform calculations, no ancestor z-index hacking. Pure CSS.



---

## Spotlight Search - Data Registry File Creation

**WHAT**: Creating the Spotlight Search data registry file — the single source of truth for all searchable items in the website builder.

**PROMPT**: Create the file `spotlight-items.ts` inside the spotlight directory of the builder components. This file should export:
1. A SpotlightCategory type for elements, actions, and components
2. A SpotlightItem interface with id, label, description, category, icon, keywords, and action fields
3. A SpotlightAction discriminated union type for insert-element, insert-component, execute, and navigate actions
4. A getStaticSpotlightItems() function returning all hardcoded items (elements from the sidebar insert panel, project-level actions like create page, publish, undo, redo, open settings, etc.)
5. A buildComponentItems() helper for dynamic component items
6. A filterSpotlightItems() function for fuzzy search across label, description, and keywords sorted by category priority

Element types must match the sidebar data format exactly. Use lucide icon names for the icon field. Follow SOURCE OF TRUTH architecture with no any/unknown types.


---

## Spotlight Search UI Component for Website Builder

**WHAT**: Create a Spotlight Search overlay component for the website builder that appears above the bottom toolbar, with keyboard navigation, search filtering, drag support for elements, and category-grouped results.

**PROMPT**: Build a SpotlightSearch component file in the spotlight directory. It should be a visual overlay positioned above the bottom toolbar (bottom: 80px, centered, z-10000). Dark theme matching the toolbar (#0a0a0a). Features: search input at top with auto-focus, results grouped by category (elements, quick actions, components), keyboard navigation (arrow keys, enter, escape), click-outside-to-close, drag support for element items, dynamic lucide icon rendering from string names, and CSS fade-in/slide-up animation. The component receives callbacks for insert-element, insert-component, execute, and navigate actions. It imports filter and item functions from a sibling spotlight-items file. Width 560px, max-height 420px, border-radius 16px. Items show icon + label, with right-side hints for navigate/execute actions.

## Template Marketplace Transaction Architecture Fix

**Prompt:** Found a bug in our template selling architecture. If the user makes money in the platform currency but their Stripe connect account currency is AED for example, and we're taking a 1% transaction fee — how will Stripe handle this? We cannot store the platform currency in our transaction DB because the transaction page shows connect account currency.

Instead, I want to:
1. Create a separate table for template marketplace transactions and a marketplace chart in the dashboard page using the platform currency, showing NET income if possible from templates.
2. We need to identify the platform fee that was charged on a specific transaction at the time of payment, because users might get different transaction fees based on their plan. We can't simply use the current plan's fee — in the past they might have had a free account with 10% fee, and later upgraded to business with 0%. Each transaction must store the percentage and platform fee that was charged at the time.
3. If transactions already store the platform fee percentage and amount, then show net income after the platform fee in the chart.
4. Add notification sound for template transactions. Currently these transactions don't show anywhere and don't notify the creator — they should send notifications just like all other connect account payments do. Fix this ASAP.

## CMS List Boolean Property Binding — Treat Empty Text as False, Non-Empty as True

Enhancement to the CMS list property evaluation system. When binding a boolean property (like visibility) to a CMS field, allow text fields to be treated as booleans via a condition — empty string evaluates to false, any non-empty text evaluates to true. This way users can bind visibility to fields like "trial_days" — if the trial field is blank (no trial), the element is hidden. If it has text like "7 days", the element shows. Same concept works for any text field where presence/absence of content should control visibility.

## Checkout Source of Truth Refactor — Eliminate Code Drift Between CMS and Standalone Add-to-Cart

I noticed something terrible. Add to cart CMS buttons add elements to the cart and the checkout element uses the CMS table to determine the trial information. But then the Add to Cart standalone system has a completely different architecture and as a result this led to code drift. If I add a product with a free trial both handle it differently. Specifically:
- Delete the createOneTimeTrialPaymentLink function
- Delete the createOneTimeTrialEmbedded function

- Keep getOrCreateStripeTrialPrice (still used by invoice service and upsell flow)
- Keep all RECURRING trial handling (that is valid Stripe behavior)

## Fix Mixed Cart Trial Bug — Subscription-Level Trial Applies to All Items

When a cart has multiple subscription products and only some have free trials, the trial_period_days is applied to the ENTIRE subscription, causing non-trial subscription items to also become trials. For example, if cart has one subscription with a 7-day trial ($100) and two subscriptions without trial ($100 each) plus a one-time product ($100), only $100 (one-time) gets charged instead of $300. The fix should split trial and non-trial recurring items into separate subscriptions, following the same secure pattern as the existing upsell flow. Security is critical — no payment intent theft, no credit card interception, no man-in-the-middle attacks between the two subscription creations. The upsell pattern is the reference architecture for the trial subscription creation after the initial payment succeeds.


---

## Mixed Cart Trial Architecture — Group by Unique Trial Period + Transaction Sync

The previous fix only split into trial vs non-trial but didn't account for multiple different trial periods (6 days, 14 days, etc.) which would still cause the same bug. The correct approach is to group recurring items by their unique trialDays value. Each group gets its own Stripe subscription. Non-trial items go in the main subscription (immediate charge), trial groups are created after payment using saved payment method (same security as upsell pattern). Also need to ensure all subscriptions from one order map to ONE transaction — use Stripe metadata with transactionId on each trial subscription so the 3-tier webhook resolver finds the right transaction. Add fallback resolvedTransactionId param to handleSubscriptionPayment. No schema changes or migrations needed.


---

## Webhook + tRPC Changes for Cart Trial-Split Subscriptions

**Prompt:**
Make changes to the webhook handlers and tRPC router. Read each file before editing.

1. In the connect webhook handler, add `subData.transactionId` as the last argument to the `handleSubscriptionPayment` call. This is needed as a fallback for trial-split subscriptions where the transactionId lives in Stripe metadata but NOT in `Transaction.stripeSubscriptionId`.

2. Check the platform webhook handler for any `handleSubscriptionPayment` calls and add the same argument if found.

3. In the payment tRPC router, add a new public endpoint `completeTrialSubscriptions` right after `createCartCheckoutSession`. This endpoint is called by the frontend after Stripe payment confirmation when the cart contained items with different trial periods. It uses `baseProcedure` since customers on published websites are not authenticated. It takes `transactionId`, `subscriptionId`, and `organizationId` as input, dynamically imports `completeTrialSubscriptions` from payment-link service, and returns the result.


---

## Frontend Trial Subscription Completion After Payment

Modify the frontend checkout and payment components (unified-checkout.tsx and unified-payment.tsx) to call `completeTrialSubscriptions` after payment succeeds when there are pending trial items. Add the tRPC mutation declaration near the existing checkout intent mutations, and invoke it after successful payment confirmation but before the receipt sessionStorage write. The trial completion is non-blocking — if it fails, the main payment already went through and the customer received their non-trial products. Also verify that the Stripe Elements mode computation correctly handles mixed trial states (main with trial + bump without trial, or vice versa) to prevent "Payment details collected in setup mode cannot be confirmed with a Payment Intent" errors.


---

## Trial Split Subscriptions for Cart and Embedded Payments

Stripe's trial_period_days applies to the entire subscription, not per-item. When a cart has recurring items with different trial states (some with trial, some without, or different trial durations), they must go in separate subscriptions grouped by unique trialDays value. Changes needed:
1. Modify handleSubscriptionPayment() to add an optional resolvedTransactionId fallback parameter for trial-split subscriptions whose IDs are not stored in Transaction.stripeSubscriptionId.
2. Modify createCartCheckoutSession() to detect mixed trial states, group recurring items by trial period, select a main group for the primary subscription, and store remaining groups as pendingTrialItems in transaction metadata.
3. Add completeTrialSubscriptions() function that creates pending trial subscriptions after main payment succeeds using the saved payment method.
4. Modify createRecurringSubscriptionEmbedded() to handle mixed trial states between main product and RECURRING order bump by splitting them into separate subscriptions.

## Refactor Transaction Architecture to Mirror Stripe's Payment Model

**Prompt:** The current Transaction model is fundamentally flawed for mixed carts. It tries to be both a payment event AND a subscription container. Stripe separates these: Payments = moment-in-time charges, Subscriptions = ongoing billing relationships. When a mixed cart has multiple subscriptions, trials, and one-time items, Stripe creates separate payment records for each charge event and separate subscription records for ongoing billing. Our Transaction model should mirror this — one Transaction = one payment event (one charge, one moment, one amount). Need a Subscription model for ongoing relationships. This is a full architectural refactor of the payment recording layer. Use teams to parallelize the work. Delete old outdated code as you change it (red-green coding) so TypeScript errors guide you to catch bugs as you code.


---

## Transaction Architecture Refactor — Service Layer

**What this prompt was used for**: Refactoring the payment/transaction architecture so that one Transaction equals one payment event instead of one entire cart checkout. A mixed cart now creates multiple Transactions linked by checkoutSessionId.

**Prompt**: 
Team A is responsible for the service layer of the transaction architecture refactor. The core change is: Before, one Transaction equaled one entire cart checkout with all items crammed into one record. After, one Transaction equals one payment event. A mixed cart creates multiple Transactions linked by checkoutSessionId.

The files owned by this team are payment-link.service.ts, transaction.service.ts, and order.service.ts. The key changes include:
1. createCartCheckoutSession - generate checkoutSessionId, group cart items by billing type, create separate Transaction records per group
2. completeTransaction - when processing payment_intent.succeeded for ONE_TIME cart payments, also mark sibling ONE_TIME transactions paid via checkoutSessionId
3. handleSubscriptionPayment - when invoice includes add_invoice_items and there's a sibling ONE_TIME transaction, mark it paid too
4. completeTrialSubscriptions - create new Transaction per trial subscription group instead of linking back to same transaction
5. createMixedBillingEmbedded - create two Transactions if main product and order bump have different billing types
6. createRecurringSubscriptionEmbedded - separate Transactions per billing group for trial mismatches
7. transaction.service.ts - add getTransactionsByCheckoutSession, update cancelSubscription for siblings, include checkoutSessionId everywhere
8. order.service.ts - add sibling transaction support, update transformOrder with siblingTransactions field

Remove pendingTrialItems and completedTrialSubscriptionIds metadata hacks since each group now has its own Transaction. Schema already has checkoutSessionId field on Transaction model.


---

This means simplifying the Amount column in transactions-table.tsx, dramatically simplifying transaction-detail.tsx by removing monthly equivalent calculations, trial metadata parsing, and complex billing type gymnastics. Also simplify orders-table.tsx and order-detail.tsx to show primary transaction amounts and checkout session context. Update unified-checkout.tsx and unified-payment.tsx to handle new response shapes with checkoutSessionId. The key principle is that each Transaction is now self-contained with its own billingType, originalAmount, trialDays, and items telling the full story without metadata hacks.

## Fix Mixed Cart Duplicate Transaction Bug

**PROMPT:**
Fix a bug in payment-link.service.ts where a mixed cart with BOTH recurring AND one-time items creates a SEPARATE ONE_TIME Transaction for one-time items AND also adds them as add_invoice_items on the subscription. This is wrong because the one-time items are charged on the subscription's first invoice via add_invoice_items, so they should be TransactionItems on the RECURRING Transaction, not a separate Transaction. 

Three changes needed:
1. In createCartCheckoutSession: When there ARE recurring items, do NOT create a separate ONE_TIME Transaction. Include one-time items as TransactionItems on the RECURRING Transaction(s). Keep one-time items' billingType as ONE_TIME. Include their amounts in originalAmount. When there are ONLY one-time items (no recurring), keep creating the ONE_TIME Transaction as before.
2. In handleSubscriptionPayment: Remove the sibling ONE_TIME transaction completion workaround code since we no longer create sibling ONE_TIME Transactions.
3. In completeTransaction: Remove similar sibling completion code if present.

Keep the add_invoice_items Stripe logic unchanged — that's correct.

## Payment/Subscription Trigger Events for Automations

We introduced subscriptions, payments with trials, and much more to the checkout system. Unfortunately there is absolutely no trigger support for this beyond a basic payment trigger that only fires when money comes through. It does not consider any events such as trial started, subscription payment received, one-time payment received, subscription cancelled, customer churned, etc. I want to add this feature to the automation builder (make sure to also feed this into the automation sequence context as well). Keep in mind there can also be a checkout element where users can pay with multiple products and prices added to cart with trials. Keep things lightweight and follow the same architecture we already accomplished correctly for transactions/subscriptions/split payments (which is technically a subscription). Please first create the plan, get my approval, and then proceed.


---

## Post-Trial Payment Incorrectly Fires TRIAL_STARTED Trigger

I noticed a bug. In the automation builder I created an automation with a TRIAL_STARTED trigger for a product. I made the payment and the trial fired as expected and the tag was added. If there were no bugs, we would get 2 payments so the Subscription Renewed trigger would fire. The Trial Started trigger SHOULD NOT FIRE because the trial ended not started. BUT the 1st renewal came in as Trial Started. All my other automations that were also listening to trial started for this product also fired, which means there is a bug. The 2nd transaction came in correctly as renewed. Please do not write bandaid code, some bad code was written earlier that needs root cause fix.

## Subscription Renewed Trigger Skips First Renewal After Trial

The first post-trial payment shows isRenewal=false when it should be treated as a renewal since the trial was the first period.

## Payment Architecture Review & Refactoring

Review all payment architecture related to payments made through the website builder payment element, payment links, and the checkout element. These features should share the exact same backend capabilities — they are simply different UI presentations of the same underlying system (mixed cart functionality).

The checkout element in the website builder is a good example of functionality that already works, but it may need refactoring for better code reuse and architectural consistency.

Review the entire implementation. There are hundreds of files and scattered pieces of payment logic, which has led to duplication and fragmented code. Another AI agent recently attempted to fix this and claimed all payment-related systems now use a clean architecture with no duplication or bugs. Verify this claim by reviewing everything again.

While reviewing:
- Identify anomalies, duplicated logic, architectural inconsistencies, and unnecessary fragmentation.
- Refactor where needed so the system follows existing architectural patterns 100%.
- Ensure the code becomes simple, readable, modular, and easy for developers to understand.
- Consolidate payment logic into a centralized architecture that can easily support future payment elements.

Main goal is modularity. For example, building an invoice payment element with the same mixed-cart functionality as the checkout element should be easy. The new architecture should make it easy to introduce new payment components without rewriting or duplicating logic.

Two modes: Test mode payments and Live mode payments. Even though their entry points may differ, they should use one shared architecture and reusable core logic whenever possible.

Special case: Payment links and the standalone payment element support split payments. Aside from that capability, everything else should remain unified with the main architecture.

Important requirements:
- Centralize and consolidate the architecture
- Remove duplicated logic
- Keep the system modular and extensible
- Make the codebase easier to read and maintain

Ensure existing systems continue to function:
- Triggers
- Notifications
- Transaction recording

These currently work well and must not break, although they may still be improved or cleaned up if the code structure requires it.

Use a team-based approach so each teammate has specialized expertise to properly handle architecture review, refactoring, and system validation.

## Refactor Invoice Payment Flows to Use Unified Checkout Architecture

Refactor `src/services/invoice.service.ts` to align with the unified checkout architecture from `src/services/payment/checkout.service.ts`. The invoice payment flows currently duplicate the entire checkout pipeline by manually resolving Stripe instances, customers, tiers, and fees. They need to use `getStripePaymentConfig()` for consistent config, `buildPaymentMetadata()` for consistent metadata, add platform fees where missing (specifically `createInvoiceOneTimeTrial()` which has no `application_fee_percent`), and document the trigger flow clearly for `completeInvoiceSubscriptionPayment()` to confirm triggers are already handled by `handleSubscriptionPayment()` in the webhook. The code should be structured to easily support test mode later even though invoices remain live-only for now. Be surgical — only modify payment flow functions, keep all CRUD functions unchanged.

## Refactor checkout.service.ts — Consolidate Transaction Creation and Clean Up Inconsistencies

Refactor the checkout service to fix internal inconsistencies:

1. Task 1 - Consolidate Transaction Creation: Several places bypass the shared createCheckoutTransaction() helper and use direct prisma.transaction.create() calls. These are in processRecurringSubscription (mixed trial state branch), processMixedBillingEmbedded (creates two transactions), createCartCheckoutSession (creates transactions directly), and completeTrialSubscriptions (creates trial group transactions). Refactor these to use the shared helper where possible, or extract into well-named private helpers.

2. Task 2 - Remove Re-exports: The backward-compatibility re-export shims for getOrCreateStripeCustomer, getOrCreateStripeProduct, getOrCreateStripePrice, etc. at line 1623-1629 should be removed after verifying no external file imports them from checkout.service.ts.

3. Task 3 - Fix createOrUpdateLead source: The createOrUpdateLead function hardcodes source as "Payment Link" for all leads, even when the source is embedded_payment or cart_checkout. Fix this to use the actual checkout source value by adding a source parameter to the function.

## Consolidate Refund Service — Backend Refactoring Agent

**Prompt:** Consolidate the refund service in `src/services/payment/refund.service.ts`. The file has two functions (`refundTransaction` and `refundSubscriptionPayment`) that are approximately 90% identical. Both do the exact same steps: find payment by stripeChargeId, idempotency check via stripeRefundId, create PaymentRefund record, recalculate payment and transaction refundedAmount from SUM, and decrement lead CLTV. The only difference is that `refundTransaction` includes items in the query and does inventory increment on full refund. Create one unified `processRefund()` function that handles both cases by always including items (lightweight no-op for subscriptions) and checking for inventory increment conditionally. Update all callers in the connect-webhook to use the new unified function directly.

## Duplicate Payment Notifications Bug

When paying for a one-time product with an order bump (both one-time), the user received 2 notifications, and both showed the TOTAL amount instead of individual amounts. This is confusing.

At an architectural level, if a checkout had 100 items, the user would get 100 notifications instead of 1 notification with the entire order.

Not proposing to change triggers — triggers still need to fire independently per product. But notifications should be a SINGLE notification per checkout/order, not per product.

Also need to consider mixed cart splits where subscription items are split across multiple Stripe subscriptions. The solution should be at the architectural level with clean, readable code following the same quality patterns as the rest of the payment system.


---

## Fix Duplicate Payment Notifications Bug — Per-Item vs Per-Checkout

I noticed a bug in the payment element on the web builder. I paid for a 1-time product with an order bump turned on, both are 1-time purchases. The user received 2 notifications, and both notifications showed the TOTAL amount instead of its individual amount. If a checkout element had 100 items the user would receive 100 notifications instead of 1. I'm not proposing that we should shift to unified architecture for all incoming purchases because we still do need to fire triggers independently etc. It doesn't make sense to send notifications if a product was purchased with multiple things. The solution should follow the same beautiful coding strategy we applied everywhere for payments — architectural level with good quality code, easy to read and understand.

## Unified Domain System — Merge Website + Email Domains

**Prompt:** Implement a unified domain system that merges the two separate domain systems (Website Domains from the Domain table for hosting, and Email Domains from the EmailDomain table for Resend email sending) into a single unified experience. When a user adds a domain, both records should be created atomically. The UI should show a single unified list with DNS records categorized as "Website DNS" and "Email DNS" within each domain's expanded view.

Decisions:
- Create both website domain and email domain at once (one-step)
- Keep both DB tables, link via domain name (no migration needed)
- Default Resend region to us-east-1 silently (no region picker in UI)

This involves:
- New unified-domain.service.ts orchestration layer
- New unified tRPC procedures added to domains router
- Rewritten domains page UI with single unified list
- Updated Mochi AI domain tools to use unified procedures
- Cleanup of dead code (email-domains-section.tsx)

## AI Generated Content Should Be Interactive Floating Canvas Elements

Make AI-generated content appear as real interactive floating canvas elements on the empty canvas, not as read-only staging previews and not inserted onto the page canvas. When AI generates UI, the content should be a floating root element that the user can immediately move, resize, and interact with — just like when they manually drag an element from the page to the empty canvas. The user should be able to freely move it on the empty canvas and change its size. Currently, staging mode makes it read-only and direct mode puts it on the page — neither is correct. We need a third placement mode (canvas-floating) that creates a floating element with directInsert to the canvas Redux store, positioned near the page in empty space.

### Mochi AI Chat — Fix All Documented Bugs
**Prompt:** Fix all the bugs documented in the Mochi AI chat architecture audit. This includes all HIGH, MEDIUM, and LOW priority items: wallet charging silent failure, no auto-send guard, website background mode content lost, placement directive ref leak, converter lazy init silent failure, background mode prompt mismatch, rapid send status flicker, event buffering race condition, JSON parse silent failures, timeout error differentiation, and SSE parse logging.


---

### Bug Fix — No Architectural Guard Against Auto-Sending Contracts/Invoices
**Prompt:** Fix Bug 2 - No Architectural Guard Against Auto-Sending Contracts/Invoices (HIGH priority). The "NEVER auto-send" rule is enforced ONLY via system prompt instructions (repeated 5+ times in caps). There is NO code-level guard preventing sendContract or sendInvoice from being called without explicit user consent. If the AI misinterprets and calls send, a contract/invoice gets sent with no undo — this is destructive and irreversible. The fix is to add a userConfirmedSend boolean parameter to both sendContract and sendInvoice tools, and add a code-level check in the execute function that blocks execution if the user has not confirmed. Read the files first to understand the tool structure, look for existing error handling patterns (handleToolError, ToolErrorResult, etc.) and follow them consistently. Add clear comments explaining WHY this guard exists.

---

### Bug Fix — Placement Directive Ref Leak and Converter Init Error Feedback in Mochi Widget
**Prompt:** Fix 2 bugs in the Mochi widget component. Write good comments above every block/function. Never use `any` or `unknown` types. Never touch git, migrations, prisma generate.

Bug 4 - Placement Directive Ref Leaks Between Rapid Consecutive Requests (MEDIUM): The `placementDirectiveRef` is updated per-request in `handleSubmit()`, but `tryInitConverter()` reads from the ref asynchronously. If two requests fire rapidly, the second request's directive overwrites the ref before the first request's converter initializes. Elements could be placed in the wrong position on the canvas. Fix: Use a Map keyed by request ID instead of a single shared ref. In handleSubmit, generate a unique ID and store the directive in the map. In tryInitConverter, look up the directive by the request's ID. Clean up the directive from the map after the converter initializes.

Bug 5 - Converter Lazy Init Has No Error Feedback (MEDIUM): If `canvasBridge.getPageInfo()` returns null repeatedly, the converter never initializes. JSONL patches arrive and are silently discarded. User sees "generating..." but nothing appears on canvas. Fix: Add a retry counter and timeout to tryInitConverter. After a reasonable limit (e.g., 10 attempts), stop retrying and surface an error using toast.error(). Message: "Failed to connect to the canvas. Please navigate to the page builder and try again." Also log a console.error with details.

---

## Fix Website Builder Buffer Replay and Event Buffering Race Condition

**Prompt:** Fix 2 bugs related to event buffering and website builder buffer replay. Write good comments above every block/function. Never use `any` or `unknown` types. Never touch git, migrations, prisma generate.

Bug 3 - Website Background Mode Content Lost - No Buffer Replay (HIGH): When a user creates a page in background mode, the page record is created (tool succeeds), but the AI-generated content (ui-spec JSONL patches) is discarded. The widget's useMochiEvents('ui-render', ...) handler processes events, but tryInitConverter() fails because no canvas/builder is mounted, so converterRef.current stays null and all patches are silently dropped. The contract builder has a working buffer replay pattern. The website builder needs the SAME pattern for 'ui-render' events. Use the BATCHED replay pattern (concatenate all deltas, process once) to avoid React maximum depth errors. Do NOT replay events one-by-one.

Bug 8 - Event Buffering Race Condition (LOW, Theoretical): In consumeFeatureBuffer(), if a builder component mounts DURING an active content stream and calls consumeFeatureBuffer(), the activeBuffering flag gets cleared. Subsequent deltas won't be buffered - they go only to live listeners. Fix: In consumeFeatureBuffer(), do NOT clear activeBuffering. Let it stay active until the complete event arrives naturally. The activeBuffering flag should only be cleared by the completion handler, not by consume.

## Fix: Stripe Metadata 500 Character Limit Bug on Ecommerce Checkout (Test Mode)

In test mode, for the ecommerce checkout element, complex payments with an order bump attached throw an error. The error is: "Metadata values can have up to 500 characters, but you passed in a value that is 827 characters." This happens because the cart items metadata JSON exceeds Stripe's 500 char limit when there are many items. Even with the order bump turned off, the checkout still fails. This is happening in production. The metadata contains product IDs, rate IDs, names, rate names, and billing types for each cart item, and when serialized it exceeds the limit.

## Mochi AI Website Builder - Responsive Card Layout Enhancement

When Mochi AI creates cards in the website builder, two things need to happen for proper responsive design:
1. The section/parent frame that holds the cards needs two different styles - on desktop view it should be side by side (horizontal), but on mobile view it needs to change to vertical alignment so cards don't get squashed.
2. The cards themselves also need two different styles - on mobile they need width fill, but on desktop they need a fixed width that is reasonable for a card. This should happen ALL the time when creating card layouts.

The AI has no guidance on layout best practices and creates brutal paddings, random shadows, and styles that dont show up in the properties panel. The AI is injecting raw CSS strings (border, boxShadow) instead of using the structured property system (__borderConfig, __effects) that the properties panel reads from. ## Mochi AI Website Builder - Extensible Preset Architecture

Follow up on the layout/properties fix. Need a proper extensible architecture for component style presets so adding new component types in the future is trivial. Refactored spec-to-canvas.ts to use a FRAME_STYLE_PRESETS map instead of nested ternaries. Each component type (Card, Alert, etc.) just adds one entry with its style overrides. All properties use the same format the properties panel expects - standard CSS properties are read directly, structured configs (__borderConfig, __effects) use specialized editors. Also fixed letterSpacing using em string instead of number which broke the panel slider.

## Mochi AI Website Builder - AI Refusing to Build Layouts (Prompt Too Restrictive)

The AI is refusing to build layouts when users show images of beautiful cards with custom styling. Instead of just building the structural approximation, it lists all the things it can't do and asks "would you like me to create the structural layout?" This is because the prompt said "You have NO style props - zero" and "do NOT try to add your own border, shadow, background" which made the AI think it should refuse requests that involve any visual design. The fix: rewrite the prompt to be empowering - ALWAYS build it, NEVER refuse, NEVER explain limitations. The AI's job is structure and content, the user customizes visual design in the properties panel after. Also added pricing card pattern with checkmark features, price display patterns, and explicit instructions to reproduce ALL content from user-provided images.

## Dynamic AI Custom Element Registry Implementation

Implement a dynamic, registry-driven system for the Mochi AI chat to support custom website builder elements (Accordion/FAQ, Video, BulletList/List, CountdownTimer/Timer). Adding a new AI-generatable element should require only one registry entry, with prompt docs, catalog definitions, and spec-to-canvas converters all auto-updating from that single entry. The system needs to support Accordion mapping to the builder's FAQ element, Video as an empty placeholder, BulletList mapping to List with generated IDs, and CountdownTimer mapping to Timer with proper segments and defaults. The registry must be server-safe (no React imports), and Accordion should reuse the existing shadcn catalog definition rather than creating a duplicate.

---

## Ecommerce Cart Cross-Site Contamination Bug (Security Breach)

Ecommerce checkout elements are populating carts with products from different websites. If a user visits Site 1 and adds XYZ products to cart but never purchases, then visits ABC website, it preloads XYZ products from Site 1. This is a security breach — carts must be scoped to the specific website/organization they belong to. Fix this ASAP.


---

## Cart Scoping Correction — Must Be Per-Website, Not Per-Org

The cart cross-site contamination fix must scope by websiteId, NOT organizationId. Organizations can have unlimited ecommerce stores (websites), so org-level scoping is still a security breach. Each website must have its own isolated cart in localStorage.


---

## Cart Scoping Fix — Use Object Map by Website ID, Don't Wipe Data

The previous cart scoping approach was overcomplicated and destructive — it wiped all cart data when switching websites. The correct approach is simple: maintain an object in localStorage mapped by websiteId, where each key holds that website's cart items. This way no data ever gets cleared, each website has its own isolated cart, and switching between websites just reads from a different key in the map. No clearing, no scope detection, just a simple keyed object.


---

## Critical Cart Bug - Cross-Website Cart Contamination & Zustand Removal

Caught a terrible bug in ecommerce checkout. The cart store architecture is fundamentally broken - it uses a single global Zustand store for the entire app. When a user visits Website A and adds products to cart, then visits Website B, they see the same products in the cart. This is a security breach - it mixes all org websites and cross-org data.

Also, the app already uses Redux, so having Zustand overly complicates the architecture for the cart. Need to:
1. Delete Zustand (verify it's not used elsewhere first)
2. Replace with Redux
3. Change the mapping to use multi-cart mapping scoped PER WEBSITE (not one global cart)
4. All other functionalities should work perfectly (stock validation, etc.)
5. Don't wipe out carts or force singleton patterns - that's bad architecture for carts. Each website gets its own isolated cart session.


---
