## Fix Build Errors (Properties Panel + Builder Settings)

**Prompt:** Fix build please.

**Context:** After implementing e-commerce template installation features (product images sync, unique page links with slug remapping, loading progress bar), the Next.js build had type errors in `properties-panel.tsx` and `builder-settings-dialog.tsx`. The errors were: (1) `selectedTable?.columns` used from `listTables` which doesn't return columns — fixed by using `tableDetails` from `getTable` query. (2) `htmlTag`, `alt`, and `ariaLabel` passed to `handlePropertyChange` which expects `keyof ResponsiveSettingsOverrides` — fixed by dispatching `updateElement` directly since these are non-responsive element properties.


---

## Fix Checkout Element Missing After E-Commerce Template Install

**Prompt:** I found one more bug, the checkout page is not fully in sync, there is not checkout element.

**Context:** The checkout page canvas data had the checkout element present, but its `orderBumpProductId` and `orderBumpPriceId` fields contained hardcoded source database IDs (`cmmo3jqv600308obee0qo8z8x`, `cmmo3ka0r00408obegornfbq7`) from the original website. These IDs don't exist in the target organization and couldn't be remapped because they weren't in the remap table. Fixed by: (1) Adding `sourceId` field to the price type in `ProductSnapshot`, (2) Adding price source ID to the Tan Utility Vest product (used as order bump), (3) Passing `remapTable` to `installProduct()` so it can map price source IDs to newly created price IDs, (4) Replacing hardcoded DB IDs in checkout canvas data with template source IDs that `remapIds()` can swap.


---

## Fix Checkout Element Type Corrupted by Slug Remapping

**Prompt:** The checkout page is not fully in sync, the checkout element is missing from the canvas — only the navbar renders.

**Context:** The checkout element existed in the seed data and was installed into the database, but its `type` field got corrupted from `"checkout"` to `"checkout-16bvq"` during the remapIds second pass. Root cause: the slug remapping added bare slug entries to the remap table (`remapTable["checkout"] = "checkout-16bvq"`), and since `remapIds()` replaces ALL matching string values in the JSON, it also matched the element's `type: "checkout"` field. Fix: removed the bare slug remap entry — only the `/`-prefixed form (`/checkout` → `/checkout-16bvq`) is added to the remap table now. All slug references in canvas data already use the `/`-prefixed form, so nothing breaks. Also fixed the corrupted data directly in the database.


---

## Fix Text Margin Not Applied in Preview/Live Mode + Add Margin to Shop Description

**Prompt:** Margin is not being applied to text in live/preview mode. Also, add 30 margin bottom to the text description paragraph on the checkout page seed.

**Context:** In unified-text.tsx, the preview mode render had `margin: 0` hardcoded AFTER the spread of `positionStyles`, which overrode any user-set margin from `computeElementPositionStyles()`. Fixed by using `positionStyles.margin ?? 0` — preserves user margin when set, resets browser defaults when not. Also added `margin: "0 0 30 0"` (30px bottom only) to the Shop Description text element in the e-commerce seed data.


---

## Fix Font Hydration Flashing on Published Pages

**Prompt:** There is a crazy hydration error on fonts because of whatever you added. It just flashes.

**Context:** Published pages load fonts twice: (1) server-side via `<link rel="stylesheet">` tags in page.tsx, and (2) client-side via `GoogleFontsService.loadFonts()` in PageRenderer's useEffect. The client-side load didn't check for existing server-rendered font links, so it appended duplicate `<link>` tags causing the browser to re-evaluate fonts and flash (FOIT). Fixed by adding a DOM check in `loadFont()` — before creating a new link, it queries `document.querySelector` for an existing Google Fonts stylesheet matching the font family. If found (from SSR), it marks the font as loaded and skips the duplicate append.


---

## Fix Text Element Hydration Errors and Font Flashing

**Prompt:** Fonts are having hydration errors. Paragraphs flash on screen. Something is wrong with text elements. It used to work perfectly before, might be because of the SEO htmlTag changes. Please fix this.

**Context:** The `htmlTag` SEO feature we added to text elements caused hydration errors on published pages. In preview mode, the code rendered a semantic tag (defaulting to `<p>`) wrapping an inner `<div>` for content styles. `<div>` inside `<p>` is invalid HTML — browsers auto-close the `<p>` before the `<div>`, creating a DOM mismatch between server-rendered HTML and client hydration, causing flashing. Fix: merged the semantic tag and content styles into a single element — the `Tag` now serves as both the position/size wrapper AND the content style container, eliminating the invalid nesting. Also changed the default from `'p'` to `'div'` to avoid the nesting issue entirely for existing elements without `htmlTag` set.


---

## Fix Text Styling Inconsistency Between Canvas and Preview Mode

**Prompt:** There are styling inconsistencies with text on canvas mode vs live preview mode. Fixed width doesn't apply in preview but works on canvas.

**Context:** After merging the semantic tag and content div into one element to fix hydration errors, `contentStyle` properties like `width: '100%'` and `position: 'relative'` (designed for the old inner-div pattern) were overriding the actual `width` from `sizeStyles` and `position` from `positionStyles` because `contentStyle` was spread last. Fix: destructure and strip layout-conflicting properties (`width`, `position`, `top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`, `overflowX`, `overflowY`, `zIndex`) from `contentStyle` before merging, keeping only visual styles (typography, colors, borders, effects).


---

## Fix Dynamic CMS Page 404 on Installed Templates

**Prompt:** In my main website clicking on a CMS item opens it in a new page and renders the data. In the new duplicated template, clicking on it also opens the page correctly, however it throws a 404. I published the page as well but it's returning a 404. Server logs show 200 but 404 is rendered.

**Context:** The SmartCMS list element stores `targetPageSlug` WITHOUT a leading slash (e.g., `"details"`). When we removed the bare slug remap entry to fix the checkout element type corruption, the `targetPageSlug` stopped being remapped. So the URL path was `/details/ProductName` but the installed page slug is `details-16bvq` — lookup fails, returns null, renders 404 (inside a 200 response). Fix: added `PROTECTED_KEYS` set to `remapIds()` — the `"type"` key is now skipped during remapping, preventing element type corruption while still allowing bare slug remapping. Restored both `/`-prefixed and bare slug entries in the remap table. Fixed the already-installed DB data.


---

## Make E-Commerce Install Loading State Minimal and Monochrome

**Prompt:** Make the installation ecommerce pages loading state minimal. Using bright colors and huge components that stand out too much. Minimal modern, no funky colors, just monochrome.

**Context:** Replaced the blue-themed progress overlay (blue borders, blue background, blue spinner, blue text, thick progress bar) with a minimal monochrome version: smaller spinner using `text-muted-foreground`, step label as `text-xs text-muted-foreground`, thin `h-1` progress bar with `bg-muted` track and `bg-foreground/40` fill. No borders, no colored backgrounds, just subtle and clean.

## Payment Elements Only Show Card in Live Mode

**Prompt:** Payment elements (checkout element and payment elements only in web builder) render only card. But in payment links, Klarna, Affirm etc show. In test mode, the web builder payment type elements perfectly render all payment methods. What happened here? Fix this but be careful not to break the multi cart checkout formats.

**Context:** After deep investigation, this is NOT a code bug. Both test and live modes use identical code with `automatic_payment_methods: { enabled: true }`. The difference is Stripe Dashboard configuration: test mode creates PaymentIntents on the platform's test account (all methods enabled by default), while live mode creates them on the connected account via `stripeAccount` parameter (only Card enabled in that account's Stripe Dashboard). Payment links work because they use the same backend. Fix: Enable Klarna, Affirm, etc. in the connected Stripe account's Dashboard under Settings - Payment Methods.

The AI needs to be aware of prebuilt components like Navbar, Sidebar, TotalMembers, LogoCarousel so it uses those actual components instead of trying to build them from scratch with basic elements.
## Klarna/Affirm 3rd Party Payment Redirect - Verification Error Bug

There is a bug with Klarna, Affirm, and other 3rd party redirect-based payments. When a lead pays with these methods, the payment completes successfully on Stripe's end (and failed payments also work correctly on Stripe's side). However, when the user is redirected back to the success page, they see a "Verification Error - No such payment_intent" message. The payment itself went through fine, but the success page cannot verify it. The redirect URL looks like: `/pay/success?payment_intent=pi_xxx&payment_intent_client_secret=pi_xxx_secret_xxx&redirect_status=succeeded&transaction_id=xxx`. Affirm's test page notes that async payments complete after redirect and integrations should rely on webhooks for payment_intent.succeeded/failed events. The success page verification is broken - likely using wrong Stripe instance (platform vs connected account) to look up the payment intent.


---

## Setup Style/Property Registry for PreBuilt Components in AI

I want to setup a registry for all the properties/styling options we have for custom/prebuilt components so that when AI streams UI it can use those properties on the components generated by AI. For example, Mochi chat does not know that prebuilt components also have border, shadow, and other more properties like that. The AI should know what style properties (backgroundColor, padding, borderRadius, borders, shadows, etc.) are available on prebuilt components and use them when generating.



## Template Purchase - Unknown Customer Bug

When someone purchases a template, they are inside the app and logged in, so we know their name from their account. They should be added as a lead into the account they bought from, and also linked to the transaction. Right now it shows as "unknown customer" because the lead/customer info isn't being passed through the template purchase flow.

## Template Approval System Bug - Always Requires Approval

The template approval system only saves the setting but doesn't actually work. It always requires approval regardless of the setting. The toggle/setting saves correctly but the actual approval logic doesn't respect the saved value.
